info.title: Release Management API - App Versions
info.description: **Looking for the old API?** You can find it here: V1 API spec The old API will be deprecated on February 1st 2027. Make sure that you have migrated to the new API before then. View the V1 → V2 Migration Guide

get.summary: Gets a list of app versions
get.description: Gives back a list of app versions.

parameters.name: items_per_page
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of app versions returned per request

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies which page should be returned from the whole result set in a paginated scenario

value.message: This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

post.description: Creates an app version under the given app.

tasks.description: After quality assurance has finished testing, check back here.

value.message: param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: name
This slack integration ID is not valid.

422.description: Unprocessable entity error for outgoing webhooks

value.message: The outgoing webhook with webhook_configuration_id '5623239f-28ea-4b8d-856e-8cb50073fade' cannot have all_events set to true while specifying events.
App Version with name "Awesome release" already exists.
This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

artifact_source.description: The source of the artifact. Can be 'ci' or 'api'.

app_id.description: The app id for the app version. An UUIDv4 identifier.

name.description: The name of your app version. In case of iOS platform this will be used in the App Store Connect for the app version and only allows X.Y.Z format

description.description: The description for your app version.

release_branch.description: The release branch part of the build configuration for the Release candidate stage.

workflow.description: The workflow part of the build configuration for the Release candidate stage.

slack_webhook_url.description: You can get notifications about release updates on Slack by setting this value. Make sure to send the properly formatted webhook url. Regex for validation: ^https:\/\/.*\.slack\.com\/services\/T[a-zA-Z0-9]*\/B[a-zA-Z0-9]*\/[a-zA-Z0-9]*$

slack_notification_integration_id.description: You can get notifications about release updates on Slack by setting this value. Make sure to send the integration ID found in Workspace Settings.

teams_webhook_url.description: You can get notifications about release updates on Teams by setting this value. Make sure to send the properly formatted webhook url. Regex for validation: ^https:\/\/(?:.*\.webhook|outlook)\.office(?:365)?\.com\/webhookb2\/.*

automatic_store_upload.description: If set to true, Release Management will upload all successful builds that ran on the given branch with the given workflow to the App Store.

release_candidate_locked.description: Set to true to lock the release candidate and prevent automatic selection of the latest artifact. When false or omitted, the latest matching artifact is selected automatically.

automation.description: Automation events (workflow or pipeline runs) setup for specific event types.

events.description: A list of event names that will trigger this webhook. These events correspond to specific actions or states in the release process.

all_events.description: Indicates whether this webhook should be triggered for all events. Set to true to enable the webhook for every event.

webhook_configuration_id.description: The unique identifier of the webhook configuration, that can be found in the workspace integration settings. This ID links the webhook configuration to the workspace.

outgoing_webhooks.description: COMING SOON. A collection of outgoing webhooks configured to trigger for specific event types. Each webhook can be associated with one or more events or set to trigger for all events.

timeline.description: Timeline settings for the app version. Dates set here will appear in the sidebar. These deadlines serve purely informational purposes, no automatic action will take place at the scheduled times.

use_presets.description: (DEPRECATED use presets_id instead) If set the release will be filled with data from previously set presets. Presets can contain release branch and workflow, automatic store upload, notification urls, approvals and automation lists. In case any of these values are set both in presets and in the release request the variables set in the request will take precedence.

presets_id.description: The presets id to be used for the release. Presets can contain release branch and workflow, automatic store upload, notification URLs, approvals, and automation lists. In case any of these values are set both in presets and in the release request, the variables set in the request will take precedence. The presets id can be retrieved using the presets listing endpoint.

tasks.description: After quality assurance has finished testing, check back here.

value.message: This slack integration ID is not valid.
The outgoing webhook with webhook_configuration_id '5623239f-28ea-4b8d-856e-8cb50073fade' cannot have all_events set to true while specifying events.

409.description: App version is scheduled or in progress

value.message: Release is in final status and cannot be modified.
App Store review is scheduled or is in progress so release candidate cannot be changed.
Release is scheduled or is in progress so release candidate cannot be changed.
This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

artifact_source.description: Determines if artifacts are coming from the Bitrise CI or from external source via API upload. Valid values are "api" or "ci". If release_branch or workflow is configured it should be "ci".

release_branch.description: The release branch part of the build configuration for the Release candidate stage.

release_candidate_id.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the artifact.

release_candidate_locked.description: Set to true to lock the currently selected release candidate and prevent automatic selection of the latest artifact.

workflow.description: The workflow part of the build configuration for the Release candidate stage.

automatic_store_upload.description: If set to true, Release Management will upload all successful builds that ran on the given branch with the given workflow to the App Store.

slack_webhook_url.description: You can get notifications about release updates on Slack by setting this value. Make sure to send the properly formatted webhook url. Regex for validation: ^https:\/\/.*\.slack\.com\/services\/T[a-zA-Z0-9]*\/B[a-zA-Z0-9]*\/[a-zA-Z0-9]*$

slack_notification_integration_id.description: You can get notifications about release updates on Slack by setting this value. Make sure to send the integration ID found in Workspace Settings.

status.description: The new status of the app version. Only the following statuses can be set externally: abandoned, completed_externally.

teams_webhook_url.description: You can get notifications about release updates on Teams by setting this value. Make sure to send the properly formatted webhook url. Regex for validation: ^https:\/\/(?:.*\.webhook|outlook)\.office(?:365)?\.com\/webhookb2\/.*

due_date.description: A valid date in the future.

approvals.description: Tasks for the approval stage. Sending this value will replace the possibly already existing approval task list.

automation.description: Automation events (workflow or pipeline runs) setup for specific event types. Sending this value will replace the possibly already existing automation list.

timeline.description: Timeline settings for the app version. Dates set here will appear in the sidebar. These deadlines serve purely informational purposes, no automatic action will take place at the scheduled times. Sending this value will replace the possibly already existing timeline.

events.description: A list of event names that will trigger this webhook. These events correspond to specific actions or states in the release process.

all_events.description: Indicates whether this webhook should be triggered for all events. Set to true to enable the webhook for every event.

webhook_configuration_id.description: The unique identifier of the webhook configuration, that can be found in the workspace integration settings. This ID links the webhook configuration to the workspace.

outgoing_webhooks.description: COMING SOON. A collection of outgoing webhooks configured to trigger for specific event types. Each webhook can be associated with one or more events or set to trigger for all events.

200.description: Updates the release settings for the version

value.message: The release type must be one of: AFTER_APPROVAL, MANUAL, SCHEDULED (case-insensitive)
Only the release_type: "scheduled" can be given an earliest release date.
The earliest release date is invalid.
Phased release setting must have a boolean value, either "true" or "false".
The app version is missing from App Store.
This endpoint is only usable for iOS releases.
This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

earliest_release_date.description: Can only be set with release_type: "scheduled". The earliest release time the version will be released after approval. Must be a valid date time string.

phased_release.description: Whether to release the version over a 7-day period or to all users immediately.

copy_primary_whats_new.description: If true, whats_new from the primary localization will be copied into other localized languages where whats_new is empty. The default value is false.

description.description: The maximum length of this parameter is 4000 characters.

keywords.description: The maximum length of this parameter is 100 characters.

promotional_text.description: The maximum length of this parameter is 170 characters.

whats_new.description: The maximum length of this parameter is 4000 characters.

percentage: must be within the valid range of 0.01-100

value.message: This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.
This endpoint is only usable for Android releases.

location.description: The time zone in which the schedule dates are provided. It can be any TZ database time zone or UTC if the dates are given in Coordinated Universal Time.

percentage.description: A number greater than 0 and less than or equal to 100 representing the release percentage, where 100 indicates a full release.

when.description: A date in ISO 8601 string format specifying when to run the schedule.

location.description: The time zone in which the schedule dates are provided. It can be any TZ database time zone or UTC if the dates are given in Coordinated Universal Time.

percentage.description: A number greater than 0 and less than or equal to 100 representing the release percentage, where 100 indicates a full release.

get.summary: Gets a list of outgoing webhooks.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUIDv4 identifier of the app version.

value.message: This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

post.description: Creates an outgoing webhook associated with the given app version.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUIDv4 identifier of the app version.

400.description: Webhook configuration is not associated with the workspace of the app version

value.message: There are missing params in the request: one of all_events or events must be present.
The given webhook configuration is not registered on the workspace.
The app version cannot be found.
A webhook configuration with id: 5623239f-28ea-4b8d-856e-8cb50073fade already exists.
This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

events.description: A list of event names that will trigger this webhook. These events correspond to specific actions or states in the release process.

all_events.description: Indicates whether this webhook should be triggered for all events. Set to true to enable the webhook for every event.

webhook_configuration_id.description: The unique identifier of the webhook configuration that can be found in the workspace integration settings.

post.summary: Submit release candidate for beta review
post.description: Submits the chosen release candidate for beta review. - IOS only

204.description: Submits the release candidate for beta review

value.message: There is no release candidate chosen for the app version.
value.details:
You can set a release candidate using the API by updating the app version with branch and workflow information.
Make sure that the latest build there contains an installable artifact.

You can check how to do this here: api/release-management/api-docs#/Releases/UpdateRelease.

Alternatively, you can also do this on the UI. Check the documentation out: https://docs.bitrise.io/en/release-management/releases/managing-the-release-process/selecting-a-release-candidate.html.

value.message: This endpoint is only usable for iOS releases.
This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

put.description: Uploads the release candidate to the Store.

value.message: This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.
